docs.title: PhD in Computer Science (NLP), Le Mans Université
docs.text: phd in computer science (nlp), le mans université doctorate on statistical machine translation in a multimodal context, supervised by prof. holger schwenk and prof. loïc barrault. established the multimodal machine-translation foundations of the research programme. 2014

docs.title: Multimodal comparable corpora for machine translation (Natural Language Engineering)
docs.text: multimodal comparable corpora for machine translation (natural language engineering) journal article (cambridge university press) formalising the use of multimodal comparable corpora to extract parallel data for mt. an early, peer-reviewed contribution to low-resource translation methodology. 2016

docs.title: Lecturer & PI at MTU; founded the Human-Centred AI Research Group
docs.text: lecturer & pi at mtu; founded the human-centred ai research group appointed lecturer in artificial intelligence and principal investigator; founded and leads the human-centred ai (hai) research group. created a research group that now hosts doctoral, postdoctoral and international members. 2018

docs.title: Arabic dialect identification & emotion detection (WANLP, SemEval-2019)
docs.text: arabic dialect identification & emotion detection (wanlp, semeval-2019) hierarchical deep learning for arabic dialect identification and the epita-adapt semeval-2019 emotion-detection system. consolidated a multilingual, arabic-focused nlp track. 2019

docs.title: ITFLOWS (H2020) — Beneficiary
docs.text: itflows (h2020) — beneficiary eu horizon 2020 project applying nlp and analytics to migration flows and public attitudes; dr afli a beneficiary and work-package contributor. first major eu consortium role; produced political-nlp datasets and deliverables. 2020

docs.title: IEEE Senior Member
docs.text: ieee senior member elevated to senior member of the ieee in recognition of professional standing. formal recognition of professional and technical contribution. 2021

docs.title: Founded and chaired the PoliticalNLP workshop series
docs.text: founded and chaired the politicalnlp workshop series general chair/editor of the workshop on nlp for political sciences (lrec 2022; lrec-coling 2024), bridging computational linguistics with governance and policy. international research leadership through a recurring, community-building venue. 2022

docs.title: gaHealth — English–Irish bilingual health corpus (LREC 2022)
docs.text: gahealth — english–irish bilingual health corpus (lrec 2022) a bilingual health-domain corpus supporting irish-language medical translation. a reusable language resource for a low-resource, high-value domain. 2022

docs.title: adaptNMT & adaptMLLM — open-source multilingual MT tooling
docs.text: adaptnmt & adaptmllm — open-source multilingual mt tooling open-source, language-agnostic environments for training and fine-tuning (multilingual) neural machine translation on low-resource languages such as irish. widely usable open-source tooling for inclusive translation research. 2023

docs.title: European patent — On-Air Split Federated Learning (WO/2024/227944)
docs.text: european patent — on-air split federated learning (wo/2024/227944) patent (filed 11 july 2024) on privacy-preserving distributed learning with positive labels, building on the group's splitfed-learning research. translated trustworthy-ai research into protected intellectual property. 2024

docs.title: External Expert, European Commission (REA)
docs.text: external expert, european commission (rea) serving as an evaluator for horizon europe research and innovation actions with the european research executive agency. recognition as an international peer evaluator of eu research. 2024

docs.title: GenDAI (Horizon Europe) — Beneficiary
docs.text: gendai (horizon europe) — beneficiary horizon europe project on generative and data-driven ai for diagnostics/health; dr afli a beneficiary at mtu. current eu project extending ai-for-health research. 2024

docs.title: Keynote — “The Language of Life”, Hologenomic Data Analysis Symposium (Brazil)
docs.text: keynote — “the language of life”, hologenomic data analysis symposium (brazil) keynote on large language models for decoding biological data, delivered at an international symposium in são carlos, brazil. international keynote linking nlp and genomics. 2024

docs.title: Media coverage — Cork Independent, “Ireland at AI ‘crossroads’”
docs.text: media coverage — cork independent, “ireland at ai ‘crossroads’” featured interview (8 may 2025) on ireland's opportunity to lead in ethical, human-centred ai, ai literacy and precision medicine. national media visibility for the human-centred ai agenda. 2025

docs.title: Findings of EMNLP 2025 — GASE and sign-language translation evaluation
docs.text: findings of emnlp 2025 — gase and sign-language translation evaluation two findings papers: generatively augmented sentence encoding, and rethinking sign-language translation evaluation. peer-reviewed *acl-venue publications in evaluation and multilingual ai. 2025

docs.title: ArabicNLP 2025 — winning PalmX system for Arabic cultural QA
docs.text: arabicnlp 2025 — winning palmx system for arabic cultural qa dual-expert and parameter-efficient llm fine-tuning for arabic islamic inheritance reasoning (qias) and arabic cultural qa (palmx shared task). culturally grounded, competition-winning multilingual ai. 2025

docs.title: Institutional Co-Lead, Rinn Artificial Intelligence at MTU
docs.text: institutional co-lead, rinn artificial intelligence at mtu institutional co-lead at mtu, deputy theme lead (inclusive language model & translation methods) and principal investigator in the national research ireland centre rinn ai. national research-centre leadership role in a national research ireland programme. 2026

docs.title: EACL 2026 — PTEB: robust text-embedding evaluation
docs.text: eacl 2026 — pteb: robust text-embedding evaluation long paper on robust text-embedding evaluation via stochastic paraphrasing at evaluation time with llms. peer-reviewed *acl publication in evaluation science. 2026

docs.title: IWSLT 2026 — robust cascaded speech translation (ADAPT–MTU HAI)
docs.text: iwslt 2026 — robust cascaded speech translation (adapt–mtu hai) shared-task system for bhojpuri–hindi and irish–english speech translation. ongoing leadership in low-resource speech translation. 2026

docs.title: Principal Investigator & MTU Lead, ADAPT Centre
docs.text: principal investigator & mtu lead, adapt centre pi and mtu institutional lead within the national research ireland adapt centre for ai-driven digital content technology. institutional leadership in a national research centre. 2021

docs.title: MA in Computational Linguistics, Université Grenoble Alpes
docs.text: ma in computational linguistics, université grenoble alpes master's in computational linguistics (thesis on arabic speech translation), supervised by prof. laurent besacier. entry point into multilingual speech and language processing. 2010

docs.title: Enterprise Ireland Innovation Vouchers (PI) with Irish SMEs
docs.text: enterprise ireland innovation vouchers (pi) with irish smes principal investigator on enterprise ireland innovation voucher awards with irish smes (bpi-unitek 2018, speire innovation 2019, interflow logistics 2021). applied research translated into sme collaborations. 2021

docs.title: LinguaAnalysis co-founder — acquired by BlueTensor (2024)
docs.text: linguaanalysis co-founder — acquired by bluetensor (2024) co-founded linguaanalysis (multilingual financial text analytics); the company was acquired in 2024 by bluetensor (italy); scientific adviser to bluetensor and emotionise.ai. research-to-commercialisation outcome in multilingual nlp. 2024

docs.title: Dr Haithem Afli joins Rinn Artificial Intelligence as Institutional Co-Lead at MTU and Deputy Theme Lead
docs.text: dr haithem afli joins rinn artificial intelligence as institutional co-lead at mtu and deputy theme lead dr haithem afli has taken on new research leadership roles within rinn artificial intelligence – research & innovation in data science and ai. he serves as institutional co-lead at mtu, deputy theme lead for inclusive language model & translation methods, and principal investigator. his work in the centre focuses on inclusive and multilingual language models, translation methods, culturally aware ai, reliable evaluation and human-centred approaches to artificial intelligence.

docs.title: New IWSLT 2026 paper on low-resource cascaded speech translation
docs.text: new iwslt 2026 paper on low-resource cascaded speech translation a paper by pournima sonawane and haithem afli was published at iwslt 2026. the work examines robust cascaded speech translation for bhojpuri–hindi and irish–english, combining whisper-based asr with nllb-200 translation and evaluating routing strategies for low-resource pairs.

docs.title: PTEB accepted at EACL 2026
docs.text: pteb accepted at eacl 2026 manuel frank and haithem afli published pteb, a dynamic text-embedding evaluation protocol using stochastic paraphrasing at evaluation time, at eacl 2026 (long papers).

docs.title: Two papers in Findings of EMNLP 2025
docs.text: two papers in findings of emnlp 2025 the hai group contributed gase (generative augmentation for sentence encoding) and a study on signer dependence in sign language translation evaluation to findings of emnlp 2025.

docs.title: Media coverage: “Ireland at AI ‘crossroads’” — Cork Independent
docs.text: media coverage: “ireland at ai ‘crossroads’” — cork independent the cork independent featured dr haithem afli on ireland’s opportunity to lead in ethical, human-centred ai — covering ai literacy and inclusion, and the use of ai in precision medicine and healthcare.
